pr.title: Defined some Game and Watch items
pr.author: djad99

summary: Defined and matched initial C implementations for several Mr. Game & Watch item article files: Greenhouse, Fire/Torch, and Breath/Sparky. The PR body reports 23 matched functions and a +0.04% overall match-rate increase. The implementations add spawn helpers, owner/fighter flag callbacks, animation-state transitions, removal checks, and simple wrapper callbacks around shared item helpers.
what_changed: Added full C contents to previously empty-looking item source files for Game & Watch Greenhouse, Fire, and Breath articles.

Implemented spawn functions for It_Kind_GameWatch_Greenhouse, It_Kind_GameWatch_Fire, and It_Kind_GameWatch_Breath using SpawnItem initialization, Item_80268B18, Item_8026AB54, db_80225DD8, and it_8027CE64 with article special attributes.
Added item callbacks that notify the owning fighter through Game & Watch attack-specific flag functions before item destruction or removal.
Implemented animation-state control callbacks using Item_80268E5C with ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE and state indices for article-specific animations.
Implemented removal-check callbacks that query owner-side fighter helpers such as ftGw_Attack11_ItemGreenhouse_CheckAll, ftGw_AttackS4_ItemCheckTorchRemove, and ftGw_AttackAirN_ItemCheckSparkyRemove.
Fire/Torch logic additionally adjusts item animation frame speed based on ftLib_800876D4(owner), writing x5D0_animFrameSpeed and applying it to the HSD_JObj with lb_8000BA0C.
Breath and Fire headers gained prototypes for fighter-side callbacks used by the item code: Sparky and Torch SetFlag/CheckRemove helpers.
smart_moves: Reused a consistent article spawn pattern across all three Game & Watch items: copy previous/current position, zero velocity, set parent gobj fields, set facing direction, set x44_flag.b0, create via Item_80268B18, then bind parent/part and special attributes.
Kept owner checks explicit before calling fighter-side callbacks, avoiding null-owner calls in item lifetime and removal paths.
Matched common item callback shapes by delegating directly to existing shared helpers such as it_8026B724, it_8026B73C, it_8026B894, Item_8026A8EC, and Item_802694CC instead of duplicating behavior.
Preserved decomp-style temporary variable naming where exact semantic names were not known, such as temp_r30 and var_r3, which is useful for matching compiler output.
Used attack-specific fighter helpers to coordinate article lifetime with the owning Game & Watch move state rather than making the item code infer fighter state directly.
decomp_lessons: Game & Watch article items often share a highly repeatable spawn-helper structure; future item decomp can use these files as templates for SpawnItem initialization and owner/article binding.
Article removal callbacks commonly return true when the owner is missing, otherwise defer to a fighter-side CheckRemove or CheckAll helper and set the owner-side flag before allowing removal.
Item animation transitions for articles may be driven by itcmd vars such as xDAC_itcmd_var0 and xDB0_itcmd_var1, followed by Item_80268E5C and Item_802694CC when an owner exists.
When item behavior depends on the owning fighter animation or state, matching may require including fighter headers or adding temporary prototypes for fighter-side helpers.
For matching, direct wrapper callbacks around shared item functions are legitimate and should not be overcomplicated: several callbacks simply forward to it_8026B724, it_8026B73C, or it_8026B894.
Weak evidence: the PR context does not include reviewer comments or detailed rationale beyond the author's claim of 23 matched functions and match-rate improvement.
naming_conventions: Game & Watch fighter-side helpers use the ftGw_ prefix and move/article-specific names, for example ftGw_Attack11_ItemGreenhouseSetFlag, ftGw_AttackS4_ItemTorchSetFlag, and ftGw_AttackAirN_ItemSparkySetFlag.
Item kind enum names follow It_Kind_GameWatch_<Article>, such as It_Kind_GameWatch_Greenhouse, It_Kind_GameWatch_Fire, and It_Kind_GameWatch_Breath.
Unidentified item functions remain address-based with the it_802Cxxxx pattern when no semantic name is established.
Item state tables remain address-named externs such as it_803F78E8 and it_803F7938.
Temporary/register-derived names such as temp_r3, temp_r30, and var_r3 are retained where they help preserve matching structure.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use chained zero assignment for vector components, as in spawn.vel.x = spawn.vel.y = spawn.vel.z = 0.0F, to match generated code shape.
Copy pos into spawn.prev_pos and then assign spawn.pos = spawn.prev_pos rather than independently assigning both from *pos.
Store item_gobj->hsd_obj in a temporary HSD_JObj pointer before animation-speed adjustment in the Fire/Torch update path.
Use explicit float constants and frame checks, for example x5CC_currentAnimFrame == 3.0f and x5D0_animFrameSpeed comparisons to 0.0f or 1.0f.
Preserve cast shapes seen in the diff, such as casting Item_GObj* to HSD_GObj* for Item_80268E5C calls in some Greenhouse callbacks.
Break out bool temporaries for removal decisions instead of returning nested calls directly, matching the var_r3 pattern.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was merged after one review according to the context, but no reviewer-specific technical feedback is available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename it_802C61F4, it_802C68F8, or it_802C720C to semantic Game & Watch article spawn names.
Check whether the temporary fighter callback prototypes added to itgamewatchbreath.h and itgamewatchfire.h were later moved into proper ftGameWatch headers.
Compare these Game & Watch article spawn helpers with other character article item spawn implementations for reusable matching templates.
Search for subsequent cleanup of temp_r3, temp_r30, and var_r3 names in these files after more type information became available.
